refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Batch update process executed at fixed intervals
Event-driven update process responding to external triggers
Parallel update streams partitioned by worksite zones or data types
Prioritized update scheduling based on operational criticality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

potential_sources: Tight coupling between update mechanism and data acquisition timing
Dependency on external event detection accuracy
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates the update process to only changed data, reducing unnecessary coupling with full model refresh processes. However, care must be taken to ensure that event detection and update triggering are well synchronized to avoid latency spikes.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable and timely detection of changes in the worksite data
Risk that incremental updates may miss some changes if event detection fails
Assumes that incremental updates can be processed within the 1.0 s latency target under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the event detection and triggering mechanism as a separate DP or sub-DP to further isolate responsibilities and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs that may trigger model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated model tasks to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication for update propagation and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical control; model update logic would diffuse heavily outside.
Partial containment possible but model updates are not primarily teleoperation functions, causing diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; model update mechanism would be poorly contained here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core system logic and model management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to update propagation.
Risk of coupling with Network Link interface causing synchronization delays.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment issues arise, consider refining the DP to separate model update triggering from update application, allowing finer modularization within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to update propagation.
Risk of coupling with Network Link interface causing synchronization delays.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental model update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the worksite model update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring compliance with safety plans and operational policies, which can be monitored via the NGHE Solution interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as this module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration, aligning well with the FR requirements.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to separate model update triggering from update application to allow finer modularization within the NGHE Solution module, which may reduce interface complexity and diffusion risks.
Monitor and manage coupling with external interfaces, especially Network Link, to mitigate synchronization delays and maintain update timeliness.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The incremental model update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, with manageable diffusion risks and clear verification feasibility. Although some recommended refinements exist, they do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage but rather suggest targeted improvements within the current level.

revise.why: Refine the DP to separate model update triggering from update application to enable finer modularization within the NGHE Solution module, reducing interface complexity and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently meets the FR latency and safety requirements.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for this functionality.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Risk of coupling with Network Link interface causing synchronization delays.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of worksite model updates (p95 ≤ 1.0 s).
Verification of compliance with safety plans and operational policies via NGHE Solution interfaces.
